quotes.text: Coaching is a great thing, because you're working for four months then you go fish for eight.
quotes.speaker: Bob Knight
quotes.context: The Indiana basketball coach joking about the perks of the coaching profession, in a roundup of his memorable lines.

source.title: CBS Sports — Bob Knight quotes: Top 10 memorable lines from Indiana's legendary but controversial coach

quotes.text: I always look in the Sunday paper to see where Everton are in the league - starting, of course, from the bottom up.
quotes.speaker: Bill Shankly
quotes.context: The Liverpool manager needling city rivals Everton, in the club's official collection of his sayings.

source.title: Liverpool FC — Bill Shankly in his own words

quotes.text: People think physicality is what separates backs at this level but it's almost never that. It's knowing where the linebacker is before you get the ball. You have to have already made three decisions by the time it touches your hands.
quotes.speaker: Barry Sanders
quotes.context: Interview with Rich Eisen on The Rich Eisen Show, October 2021
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Sanders consistently downplays raw athleticism and emphasizes vision and anticipation when he analyzes running back play, which is his real refrain in interviews. The subtle tell is 'almost never,' which is a touch more assertive than the genuinely modest Sanders usually gets.

quotes.text: People ask me what I was thinking on that last drive and honestly I wasn't thinking anything, that's the whole point. The moment you start narrating it to yourself you're already a half-second behind and in this league a half-second is the whole game.
quotes.speaker: Tom Brady
quotes.context: Interview with Jim Gray on the 'Let's Go!' podcast, September 2022
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Brady really does talk about mental processing and the zone state in almost exactly this cadence, so a Patriots or Bucs fan would nod right along. The subtle giveaway is 'narrating it to yourself,' which is just a touch too clean and introspective for his usual blunter phrasing.

quotes.text: I've been in locker rooms after losses where you can hear every individual heartbeat and I've been in ones where it's already over before anyone even sits down. That second kind is the thing that scares me the most as a coach.
quotes.speaker: Doc Rivers
quotes.context: Postgame press conference following a Philadelphia 76ers playoff elimination, ESPN broadcast, May 2023
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Rivers talks about team culture and collective resilience in exactly this evocative, experience-drawing style, and the locker room is his natural metaphor. The tell is the poetic compression of it; his actual postgame quotes meander more and repeat themselves.

quotes.text: When I was coming up, older guys in the clubhouse didn't explain things to you, they just let you fail until you figured it out. Some guys never figured it out. I don't know if that's wisdom or cruelty, maybe both.
quotes.speaker: Derek Jeter
quotes.context: The Captain, ESPN documentary series, episode 5, aired July 2022
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Jeter reflects on the old-school Yankees clubhouse culture in exactly this measured, slightly ambivalent tone throughout the actual documentary. The tell is the 'maybe both' kicker; Jeter in reality almost always lands on the affirming side rather than leaving things genuinely unresolved.
